sources.notes: Simulate k cyclic pair comparisons between arrays a and b with k up to 1e18.
Detailed solution reduces values to thresholded 0/1 states, groups by gcd(n,m), and maintains useful tokens with segment tree prefix minima and priority queues.

annotation.summary: Compute the final state after repeatedly comparing a[i mod n] and b[i mod m] and swapping if the a value is larger.
annotation.constraints: n+m totals 2e5 but k can be 1e18, so direct simulation is impossible.
annotation.core_idea: Process values in increasing order and reduce the question to a 0/1 process at each threshold. Only pairs where a has 1 and b has 0 cause changes. Split indices by gcd(n,m), reorder each class along the cycle generated by +m mod n, and view potential changes as tokens moving forward. Extra tokens at a position are pushed to the next positions; tokens whose effective time exceeds K no longer affect the final state. The active-token structure is equivalent to matching parentheses on prefix sums of counts c_i-1; segment tree prefix minima locate the affected segment, while priority queues keep the largest-time token at each segment start.

solution_variants.name: token-cycle-segment-tree
solution_variants.summary: For each gcd class, maintain active tokens under cycle pushing using a lazy segment tree over prefix minima and per-position priority queues.

tags.evidence: A lazy segment tree maintains prefix minima and finds the segment affected by each added token.
Priority queues store token times at each position so the largest active token can be removed when a segment overflows.
tags.description: Heap/priority-queue techniques for repeatedly retrieving current minimum or maximum candidates under incremental updates.
tags.created_reason: Priority queues are a standard reusable data-structure tag and appear as a nontrivial component here.

tags.evidence: Values are processed in increasing order to reconstruct final threshold crossings.
The process splits into independent residue classes modulo gcd(n,m), then each class is reordered as a single cycle.
